text:
Your working directory is a checkout of the real react-datepicker repository (a React date-picker component library) at the commit where the bug below reproduces. Find and fix the bug in the library source.

Bug: in a range-selection date picker (e.g. `selectsRange`) that displays more than one month at a time, after a start date has been chosen the user hovers over a date to preview the range that would be selected. Each month's calendar grid also renders a few 'filler' day cells that actually belong to the adjacent month (the leading days from the previous month and the trailing days from the next month, shown to fill out the first/last week rows). The bug is that these out-of-month filler cells incorrectly receive the in-selecting-range preview highlight. A day cell that falls outside the month its grid represents should never participate in the selecting-range highlight — only cells that genuinely belong to the displayed month should. Fix the day-rendering logic so the in-selecting-range highlight is applied only to cells belonging to the month being displayed. Fix the library source (under `src/`); do not just add workarounds in test files.

Environment notes: this is a Node/TypeScript project managed with Yarn (Berry) via Corepack; dependencies are already installed (re-run `corepack enable && yarn install --immutable` if you ever need to). Tests use Jest — run a single file with `node_modules/.bin/jest <path>`.

/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,45p' src/test/day_test.test.tsx; sed -n '430,520p' src/month.tsx; sed -n '120,240p' src/month.tsx; sed -n '1190,1270p' src/test/day_test.test.tsx; sed -n '1,220p' src/test/multi_month_test.test.tsx"
/bin/bash -lc "sed -n '1,220p' src/week.tsx; rg -n \"monthsShown|showPreviousMonths|monthShows\" src/calendar.tsx src/index.tsx src/month.tsx | head -100; sed -n '1,140p' src/day.tsx"

text: The issue is in `Day.isInSelectingRange()`: it evaluates the actual date without excluding the cells marked as outside the displayed month. The component already has `isAfterMonth()`/`isBeforeMonth()` helpers for those filler cells, so I’ll use that same month-membership check before applying the preview range logic.
